sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 0.4g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a tuber, glycemic behaviour matters a ton.
sections.subtitleText: 0.4g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a tuber, glycemic behaviour matters a ton.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 27.8g of carbs. glycemic index is where this starts to get messy. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.
sections.subtitleText: 27.8g of carbs. glycemic index is where this starts to get messy. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 1.4g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.
sections.subtitleText: 1.4g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin c at 17% daily value, vitamin a at 0% daily value stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin c at 17% daily value, vitamin a at 0% daily value stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: minerals are basically not part of the case here.
sections.subtitleText: minerals are basically not part of the case here.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: practical staple carb source because tubers are often judged by how usable they are day to day. whole-food carb package because that matters because it is a cleaner baseline than heavily refined carbs. can be quite filling because a useful practical strength when the preparation stays sensible.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: on the weaker side: still a carb-heavy category because that is the core ceiling tubers have to manage. preparation changes outcomes a lot because what you do to a tuber can make or break it. protein support is weak because tubers still need help from the rest of the meal.

closing.summary: Cassava (Boiled) does have a few real-world upsides. but the nutrition case is still weak and the downsides drag it down hard. best only when you want easy carbs and can accept the missing upside.
closing.useCaseNote: Best only when you want easy carbs and can accept the missing upside.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in D tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in D tier with an overall score of 22 from a base nutrition score of 22. Context barely changed the result.
